Classification: Restricted (V2) 
Summer Practice 2026 
 
🐾 PetPal (Lost & Found Pets) 
Goal: A user-friendly app for reporting, browsing, and reuniting lost and found pets in a 
local area - dogs, cats, birds, and other animals. Users can post photos to help identify 
and locate missing pets. 
 
General functional requirements: 
● Landing page (to be displayed when the user is not signed in) 
○ Show the name + functional description of the application 
○ Display a few relevant images (can be g

Show the name + functional description of the application 
○ Display a few relevant images (can be generated with AI) 
○ Log in form: 
▪ Email address, password 
▪ Login button 
▪ Forgot password link (without implementation, later stage) 
○ Create account button (without implementation, later stage) 
● Header 
○ Include the name of the authenticated user 
○ Dropdown with Logout functionality 
● Navigation menu 
● ‘Dashboard’ page 
○ Display cards that are used for accessing functionalities from

avigation menu 
● ‘Dashboard’ page 
○ Display cards that are used for accessing functionalities from within the app. 
○ Add a carousel with the latest images added (recipes, animals, etc) 
● ‘About us’ page 
○ Presentation of team members with pictures, names, and short descriptions. 
○ Application version and short technical description (stack suite, architecture, 
etc) 
● ‘Contact us’ page 
○ Later stage: Include a page that enables users to fill in a Google/Microsoft form 
(embed code, no aut

ter stage: Include a page that enables users to fill in a Google/Microsoft form 
(embed code, no auth required)  
● Roles:  
○ Users  
○ Later stage: Administrator role - Upon logging in, users identified by their email 
address are granted elevated access. Administrators can perform all end-user 
actions, plus: full CRUD on all content, moderation photos, and access to an 
admin dashboard with charts

Classification: Restricted (V2) 
🧩 Core functionalities 
Report list 
 
Display in a list (table/cards), at least 6 tiles/panels with website-specific pet 
reports:  
 
Title (e.g. "Lost: tabby cat near Central Park"), location last seen, date, 
short description, image. 
 
Status (e.g. lost, found, reunited). 
 
Use pagination to navigate through the reports.  
 
Sort by default based on the latest added report.  
 
Use filters (status, animal type, area).  
 
Search by keyword o

d on the latest added report.  
 
Use filters (status, animal type, area).  
 
Search by keyword or location. 
 
Display on top ‘Add report’ button 
 
add a new record in DB via a form 
 
If successful => go back to the list and see new record 
 
Report details page 
 
Display report details in a structured read-only layout: title, status, animal 
type, location last seen, date, description, contact details, image.  
 
Display a gallery in a carousel/dialog of user-submitted photos for th

contact details, image.  
 
Display a gallery in a carousel/dialog of user-submitted photos for that report.  
 
Upload or take a photo and attach it to the report. 
 
Edit report from details page 
 
Display the fields in a form. 
 
Add the ‘Edit report’ button. 
 
Cancel button returns from Edit State to details page without saving. 
 
Edit/Delete button available only for the "own" report. 
 
Mark report as resolved/reunited (own reports only). 
 
Photo functionality 
 
Upload an ima

t. 
 
Mark report as resolved/reunited (own reports only). 
 
Photo functionality 
 
Upload an image from the device, or take a photo directly using the device 
camera (where supported) 
 
Validate file type and size before uploading (e.g. JPG/PNG, max size limit 
5MB).  
 
Preview the photo before submitting, with the option to remove/replace it.  
 
Attach multiple photos to a single report.

💡 Stretch Features (If time allows) 
 
Classification: Restricted (V2) 
 
Generate a printable “missing pet” poster (PDF) from a report 
 
Social media sharing buttons 
 
Map view showing report locations 
 
Email notification when a new report matches a watchlist area 
 
Apply basic image editing (crop / rotate) before uploading 
 
Leave a comment / tip on a report (e.g. "I think I saw this dog on Main St").
